title: Fix incorrect multiplier for limit field
body:
This PR corrects how the query cost multiplier is applied when a limit input is involved. We recently introduced a limit parameter for values on some fields (notably Attribute.values). The multiplier logic was inadvertently affecting the wrong place (SelectedAttribute-related path). The change ensures that the limit-based cost multiplier is only counted where pagination actually happens and does not inflate costs for non-paginated fields like SelectedAttribute.values.

Key points:
- The SelectedAttribute type should not contribute to query cost via a limit multiplier (it is not paginated).
- Limit-based multipliers should apply to the Attribute.values path (where pagination exists) and not to SelectedAttribute.values.
- No behavioral changes to how values are resolved, just the cost-multiplier application path.

Proposed behavior after this PR:
- Cost calculation for queries involving SelectedAttribute.values should not consider any limit-based multiplier.
- Cost calculation for Attribute.values should continue to respect its limit multiplier as intended (when present in cost map definitions).

step_plan.summary: This PR fixes misapplied limit-based cost multipliers by ensuring only paginated fields (Attribute.values) incur cost multipliers, and SelectedAttribute.values are excluded.

steps.operation: Refactor get_args_from_cost_map to ignore limit-based multipliers for non-paginated fields
steps.reason: Ensure multipliers derived from the cost map are only applied to fields that actually paginate; prevent cost inflation from SelectedAttribute.values.
steps.side_effects: Requires tests to validate no cost increase for SelectedAttribute.values; may affect tests around query cost; ensures future limit multipliers are isolated to Attribute.values.

steps.operation: Update compute_cost to utilize the guard in multiplier application and ensure only paginated paths influence cost
steps.reason: Align compute_cost with the guarded multiplier application; ensure changes propagate to all cost calculations and that non-paginated paths are excluded from limit multiplier impact.
steps.side_effects: Potential test adjustments; ensure performance unaffected; ensure no regression for Attribute.values multiplier behavior; need tests for the new behavior.
